questions.question: What is the main source of energy on Earth?
questions.explanation: All plants and animals are dependent on it.

questions.question: Why do we use soil for planting vegetables?
questions.correct_answer: Soil provides sufficient air and space for roots to grow easily.
questions.explanation: Bhoomi and Surya helped Ajji in preparing the soil for planting vegetables.

questions.question: What is formed by the disintegration of rocks over a long time?
questions.explanation: The process takes thousands to millions of years.

questions.question: Why do earthworms help in turning and loosening the soil?
questions.correct_answer: They are natural agents that help in turning and loosening the soil.
questions.explanation: Bhoomi and Surya observed earthworms in the soil.

questions.question: What is conserved and used responsibly due to its formation taking thousands to millions of years?
questions.explanation: Rocks play a vital role in our lives.

questions.question: What are the different types of fuels used by vehicles?
questions.explanation: Different types of vehicles use different types of fuels.

questions.question: Why do we need to conserve minerals found in nature?
questions.correct_answer: We need to conserve them due to their formation taking thousands to millions of years.
questions.explanation: Rocks and minerals are transported from one location to another.

questions.question: What is the importance of recycling in nature?
questions.correct_answer: The leaves that fall from trees decay and enrich the soil with nutrients.
questions.explanation: This helps new plants and trees grow.

questions.question: Why do we use heat and light from the Sun for various purposes?
questions.correct_answer: We use it for activities like drying clothes, making food, etc.
questions.explanation: Bhoomi drew pictures to show the uses of heat and light from the Sun.

questions.question: What is World Water Day observed on?
questions.explanation: It's an important day for water conservation.

important_notes.title: Natural Resources
important_notes.content: Natural resources are essential to sustain all life forms on Earth. We get them from nature and use them to make many useful things for our convenience.

important_notes.title: Soil Formation
important_notes.content: Soil is formed by the disintegration of rocks over a long time, taking thousands to millions of years.

important_notes.title: Rocks and Minerals
important_notes.content: Rocks play a vital role in our lives, and minerals found in nature are essential for various activities.

important_notes.title: Fossil Fuels
important_notes.content: Fossil fuels like petrol and diesel are used by vehicles for transportation.

important_notes.title: Recycling in Nature
important_notes.content: The leaves that fall from trees decay and enrich the soil with nutrients, helping new plants and trees grow.

important_notes.title: Importance of Sun's Energy
important_notes.content: We use heat and light from the Sun for various activities like drying clothes, making food, etc.

important_notes.title: Conserve Natural Resources
important_notes.content: We need to conserve natural resources responsibly due to their formation taking thousands to millions of years.

important_notes.title: Soil Characteristics
important_notes.content: Soil provides sufficient air and space for roots to grow easily, making it essential for plant growth.

important_notes.title: Earthworms' Role
important_notes.content: Earthworms are natural agents that help in turning and loosening the soil, making it suitable for plant growth.

important_notes.title: Minerals Found in Nature
important_notes.content: Naturally occurring minerals like gold, silver, copper, cobalt, etc. are essential for various activities.

facts.fact: The Sun is the main source of energy on Earth.
Soil is formed by the disintegration of rocks over a long time, taking thousands to millions of years.
Rocks play a vital role in our lives and are essential for various activities.
Minerals found in nature are essential for various activities, including construction, electricity generation, etc.
Fossil fuels like petrol and diesel are used by vehicles for transportation.
The leaves that fall from trees decay and enrich the soil with nutrients, helping new plants and trees grow.
Earthworms are natural agents that help in turning and loosening the soil, making it suitable for plant growth.
Naturally occurring minerals like gold, silver, copper, cobalt, etc. are essential for various activities.
The importance of recycling in nature should be understood to conserve natural resources responsibly.
World Water Day is observed on 22nd March every year to raise awareness about water conservation.
